question: Elder Scrolls VI released by December 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Elder Scrolls VI is officially released in the US by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source will be official information from Bethesda Softworks or its parent company, Microsoft.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors "No" at 82.5% for Elder Scrolls VI releasing by December 31, driven by Bethesda's silence on any near-term timeline amid its focus on Starfield's Shattered Space DLC and Fallout updates post-Amazon TV success. Announced in 2018 with a brief teaser, the open-world RPG remains in early-to-mid development per Todd Howard's recent IGF comments, echoing Skyrim's eight-year cycle and Starfield's delays. No marketing blitz or holiday showcase nods from Xbox signals impossibility, as massive titles require months of hype; historical Bethesda patterns point to 2026-2028 at earliest. Game Awards on December 12 looms as the final catalyst, but traders see slim odds of a surprise drop.

question: Will Starmer say "Mr Speaker" 20+ times during the next Prime Minister's Questions event?
description:
Keir Starmer is scheduled to participate in the Prime Minister's Questions (PMQs) in the Commons Chamber in the Palace of Westminster on every Wednesday. (https://www.parliament.uk/visiting/visiting-and-tours/watch-committees-and-debates/prime-ministers-questions/).

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Keir Starmer says the listed term during the next Prime Minister's Question Time event he participates in. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Any usage of the term regardless of context will count toward the resolution of this market.

Pluralization/possessive of the term will count toward the resolution of this market, however other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

If no such Prime Minister's Question Time event happens by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No". If Starmer ceases to be Prime Minister for any length of time by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

This market is explicitly about scheduled events featuring Keir Starmer titled "Prime Minister's Question Time" (https://www.parallelparliament.co.uk/mp/keir-starmer/dept/cabinet-office/calendar). Speeches, events, or comments that occur outside of named, scheduled events will not qualify toward this market's resolution. If the event contains a Q&A, it will count toward the resolution of this market.

The resolution source will be video of the events. Only remarks which are broadcast or streamed live will count toward this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Keir Starmer's statements at the next Prime Minister's Questions session on October 23 hinges on fallout from Labour's contentious welfare bill, where over 50 MPs rebelled against cuts to winter fuel payments before Starmer secured a narrow win on October 22. Opposition Leader Kemi Badenoch has hammered fiscal austerity and foreign policy missteps, including Middle East tensions, in recent clashes. Odds favor Starmer reiterating economic discipline and party unity defenses, with sentiment shifting post-rebellion as his authority faces tests. The Autumn Statement on October 30 looms as a catalyst for budget previews that could shape his prepared responses amid tightening polls.

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

If no such Prime Minister's Question Time event happens by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No". If Starmer ceases to be Prime Minister for any length of time by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

This market is explicitly about scheduled events featuring Keir Starmer titled "Prime Minister's Question Time" (https://www.parallelparliament.co.uk/mp/keir-starmer/dept/cabinet-office/calendar). Speeches, events, or comments that occur outside of named, scheduled events will not qualify toward this market's resolution. If the event contains a Q&A, it will count toward the resolution of this market.

The resolution source will be video of the events. Only remarks which are broadcast or streamed live will count toward this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Keir Starmer's statements at the next Prime Minister's Questions session on October 23 hinges on fallout from Labour's contentious welfare bill, where over 50 MPs rebelled against cuts to winter fuel payments before Starmer secured a narrow win on October 22. Opposition Leader Kemi Badenoch has hammered fiscal austerity and foreign policy missteps, including Middle East tensions, in recent clashes. Odds favor Starmer reiterating economic discipline and party unity defenses, with sentiment shifting post-rebellion as his authority faces tests. The Autumn Statement on October 30 looms as a catalyst for budget previews that could shape his prepared responses amid tightening polls.

question: Daniel Noboa out as President of Ecuador by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Daniel Noboa ceases to be President of Ecuador for any period of time between market creation and the specified date (Ecuador Time, GMT-5). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Daniel Noboa's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Daniel Noboa and the government of Ecuador; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Daniel Noboa's dominant position in Ecuador's 2025 presidential polls anchors the 94% "No" odds against him exiting office by June 30, reflecting trader confidence in his re-election prospects. Recent surveys from October 2024 place him well ahead of rivals, fueled by sustained high approval for his security crackdown on gangs, including a decisive April referendum win authorizing military prison oversight and extraterritorial operations. Absent any impeachment momentum in the National Assembly or judicial rulings to shorten his term ending May 2025, markets anticipate continuity through the February 9 first-round vote and potential runoff. This skin-in-the-game consensus highlights stability amid ongoing violence reduction efforts.

question: Will "Ready or Not 2: Here I Come" score at least 73 on the Rotten Tomatoes Tomatometer?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the displayed Rotten Tomatoes “All Critics” Tomatometer score for Ready or Not 2: Here I Come (2026) is at least equal to the specified number at 10:00 AM ET on March 23, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If, for any reason, the resolution data is unavailable at this market's specified end time, the resolution source will be checked until the relevant data is available. This market will resolve to “No” if no data is available by March 27, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment for "Ready or Not 2: Here I Come" Rotten Tomatoes score leans bullish, driven by the 2019 original's Certified Fresh 89% acclaim and returning star Samara Weaving alongside directors Radio Silence, fresh off "Abigail's" solid reception. Horror sequels thrive in today's market—witness "Smile 2" hitting 79%—but critics often ding follow-ups for lacking novelty, capping implied probabilities around 75-85%. No reviews exist yet as production wraps without a firm release date, making trailer drops and festival premieres key catalysts; first critic verdicts post-embargo could shift odds dramatically, echoing volatile pre-release swings like "Barbarian's" surprise surge.

question: Will Trump be impeached before his term ends?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes“ if the US House of Representatives, by simple majority vote, approves or passes one or more articles of impeachment of President Donald Trump between market creation and January 20, 2029, at 12:00 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Neither trial nor conviction by the US Senate, nor removal from office, is necessary to resolve this market to “Yes“.

The primary resolution source for this market will be information from the federal government of the United States; however, a consensus of credible reporting will be used.

events.title: Will Trump be impeached before his term ends?
events.description:

Neither trial nor conviction by the US Senate, nor removal from office, is necessary to resolve this market to “Yes“.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus pricing a 55.5% yes probability for Donald Trump facing impeachment before his second presidential term ends in 2029 hinges on the GOP's narrow House majority—projected at roughly 220-215 seats—leaving room for Democratic initiatives or Republican defections amid controversies. Trump's two prior impeachments set a precedent of heightened scrutiny, amplified by recent cabinet nomination turbulence, including Pete Hegseth's confirmation challenges over past allegations and Matt Gaetz's withdrawal as attorney general pick. No active articles of impeachment advance in the incoming 119th Congress, but traders factor in risks from policy clashes, potential investigations, and partisan tensions as the lame-duck session wraps and January 2025 inaugurations approach.

question: Aliens.gov confirmed immigration website?
description:
On March 18, 2026, rumors emerged that the U.S. federal government had registered the domains “aliens.gov” and "alien.gov."

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the U.S. government officially announces or confirms that “aliens.gov” or "alien.gov" is intended for immigration-related purposes, or if the website becomes publicly accessible and its content is clearly and predominantly related to U.S. immigration or information for or about non-citizens (“aliens”), by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For announcements to qualify, they must be official. For confirmations to qualify, they must be unambiguous, on-the-record public statements issued by an authorized governmental person or entity, speaking in an official capacity.

If either website becomes publicly accessible and its content is clearly and predominantly related to extraterrestrial/UFO topics or any subject not primarily related to immigration, or if any qualifying announcement or confirmation establishes that the domains are intended for such non-immigration-related purposes, this market will resolve to "No".

Informal statements, placeholder pages, inactive domains, or any content that is not clearly indicative of the domains' primary purpose will not be considered.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of the United States; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Absence of any official confirmation from federal agencies or the Trump transition team has driven the 86% "No" consensus on a confirmed aliens.gov immigration website. President-elect Trump's campaign trail mention of creating such a tracker for undocumented immigrants and deportations generated initial buzz, but no primary announcements, executive actions, or Department of Homeland Security plans have materialized since his November victory. Recent headlines emphasize broader mass deportation strategies, military involvement, and policy task forces, yet lack specifics on digital infrastructure like aliens.gov. Traders weigh this against inauguration on January 20 and historical delays in new admin tech rollouts, viewing confirmation as improbable absent concrete developments.

question: Will Alberto Pimentel be in the top 3 of Big Brother Brasil 26?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed contestant is in the final 3 players of Big Brother Brasil 26.

If Big Brother Brasil 26 concludes without a winner being declared, or if Big Brother Brasil 26 has otherwise not concluded by May 31, 2026, 11:59PM ET this market will resolve to "No". In the case of a tie for any relevant ranking, this market will resolve to the names of listed contestants involved in the tie. 

This market will remain open until the conclusion of the season.

The resolution source for this market will be the official broadcast of Big Brother Brasil 26.

question: Will Charles Hittler be the next mayor of Arcis-sur-Aube?
description:
The second round municipal election to elect the municipal council of Arcis-sur-Aube is scheduled to take place on March 22, 2026; the elected councillors will then choose the mayor.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who becomes the next elected Mayor of Arcis-sur-Aube.

Temporary, interim, or placeholder mayors appointed before the election will not be considered.

This market includes any potential second round. If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the French Government, specifically the Ministry of the Interior (https://www.interieur.gouv.fr/).

events.title: Hittler vs. Zielinski: Arcis-sur-Aube Mayoral Election Winner

eventMetadata.context_description: Charles Hittler's near-certain trader consensus at 99.8% in the Arcis-sur-Aube mayoral election stems from his incumbency as the Rassemblement National mayor, who secured 52.8% in the 2020 runoff against Annie Soucat in this small Aube commune tied to Marine Le Pen's origins. Local dynamics favor continuity, with no recent polls, scandals, or endorsements elevating challengers Antoine Renault-Zielinski or Soucat, reflecting stable voter base rates for incumbents in French municipal races. Realistic shifts could involve a high-profile opposition alliance, personal controversy impacting Hittler, or national RN momentum changes before the March 2026 first-round vote, though traders price these as remote amid quiet campaign phases.

question: Will Donald Trump visit Ireland in 2026?
description:
If U.S. President Donald Trump visits a listed country between the creation of this market and December 31, 2026 11:59 PM ET, the relevant market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purpose of this market, a "visit" is defined as Trump physically entering the terrestrial or maritime territory of the listed country. Whether or not Trump enters the country's airspace during the timeframe of this market will have no bearing on a positive resolution.

The primary resolution source for this information will be official information from government of the United States of America, official information from Trump or released by his verified social media accounts (e.g. https://twitter.com/POTUS), however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket for Donald Trump's 2026 international visits hinges on his first-term patterns and emerging second-term foreign policy signals, with no official itinerary announced as of early 2025. High implied probabilities favor traditional allies like Saudi Arabia—site of his 2017 debut trip—and Israel amid ongoing Middle East tensions, while Mexico and Canada reflect trade and border priorities under USMCA renewal talks. Recent cabinet picks, including foreign policy hawks like Marco Rubio for State, boost odds for confrontational destinations like China or Ukraine, but geopolitical flux from Ukraine aid debates and NATO summits could shift dynamics. Key upcoming events include the G7 summit (host TBD) and potential bilateral deals post-inauguration.

description:

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by Unwell Winter Games rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from Unwell; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: Will there be a perfect NCAA bracket?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if anyone completes a perfect bracket for the 2026 Men's Basketball NCAA Tournament on any of the following websites: ESPN, NCAA.com, CBS Sports, Yahoo Fantasy, Fox Sports, Sports Illustrated, USA Today, Kalshi. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A perfect bracket is defined exclusively as a bracket that selects the correct outcome of every game during every round of the 2026 Men's Basketball NCAA Tournament. A perfect bracket only qualifies for a completed tournament. Perfect brackets for incomplete or partially completed tournaments will not be considered. No form of ‘second chance’ bracket will be considered. Only brackets that can be verified as having been submitted before the start of the first Round of 64 game will be considered.

If the 2026 Men's Basketball NCAA Tournament is cancelled, postponed after April 20, 2026, 11:59 PM ET or the 2026 Men's Basketball NCAA Tournament matchups have not been declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source will be official information from ESPN, NCAA, CBS Sports, Yahoo Fantasy, Fox Sports, Sports Illustrated, USA Today, or Kalshi.

eventMetadata.context_description: The 2024 NCAA Men's Basketball Tournament concluded on April 8 with UConn's championship, and no verified perfect bracket emerged from millions tracked by ESPN, Yahoo, or major pools like Warren Buffett's challenge, driving trader consensus to 99.6% "No." Historical precedent reinforces this—zero perfect brackets in the 38-year modern era amid frequent upsets, Cinderella runs, and the 1-in-120-billion odds for random picks—leaving even bracketology experts stumped after Round 1's chaos. While unverified claims in obscure private pools could theoretically surface, official verifications and the tournament's finality make a reversal improbable, cementing the wisdom of crowds in current pricing.

question: Will FC Andorra win on 2026-04-01?
description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for April 1, 2026
If FC Andorra wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: FC Andorra vs. Málaga CF
events.description: This event is for the upcoming La Liga 2 game, scheduled for Wednesday, April 1, 2026 between FC Andorra and Málaga CF.

eventMetadata.context_description: Málaga CF's impressive recent form—unbeaten in five La Liga 2 matches including road wins—clashes with FC Andorra's stubborn home defense at high-altitude Estadi Nacional, keeping trader probabilities dead-even at 50% across win, draw, and Málaga victory. Andorra (14th) has drawn three of their last five, leveraging rest advantages post-international break, while third-placed Málaga boasts a perfect head-to-head record but faces travel fatigue. Official injury reports show no major absences for either side, amplifying uncertainty in this mid-table scrap where upsets thrive amid playoff positioning pressures and evenly split momentum.

events.description: This event is for the upcoming La Liga 2 game, scheduled for Wednesday, April 1, 2026 between Burgos CF and AD Ceuta FC.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ceuta FC's red-hot form as leaders of Primera Federación Group 2, with an eight-match unbeaten streak including cup upsets over higher-division sides, has traders pricing this Copa del Rey round-of-32 clash neck-and-neck despite Burgos CF's home-field edge in Segunda División. Burgos sits mid-table after a mixed run of two wins in five, vulnerable to lower-tier surprises in knockout play where crowd support often falls short against motivated underdogs. No key injuries reported for either squad ahead of January 7, leaving the market's even split reflecting Ceuta's momentum neutralizing Burgos' league pedigree and venue advantage, with draws common in such cross-tier ties.

events.title: SD Huesca vs. Cultural y Deportiva Leonesa
events.description: This event is for the upcoming La Liga 2 game, scheduled for Wednesday, April 1, 2026 between SD Huesca and Cultural y Deportiva Leonesa.

eventMetadata.context_description: SD Huesca's home advantage at El Alcoraz clashes with Cultural Leonesa's red-hot form, fueling the razor-thin trader consensus across win, draw, and away upset outcomes. As a Segunda División side, Huesca holds divisional superiority but enters on a three-match winless skid, conceding freely in recent league outings amid injury concerns for midfield anchors like Óscar Sielva. Leonesa, topping Primera Federación Group 1, boasts an eight-game unbeaten streak with potent counterattacks led by late bloomer Álvaro Padilla. Copa del Rey history favors upsets for lower-tier squads on the road, while Huesca's rest edge post-midweek could tip scales—yet defensive lapses keep probabilities tightly bunched near equilibrium.

events.title: Real Racing Club vs. Real Sporting de Gijón
events.description: This event is for the upcoming La Liga 2 game, scheduled for Wednesday, April 1, 2026 between Real Racing Club and Real Sporting de Gijón.

eventMetadata.context_description: Racing Club's home strength at El Sardinero, where they've won four of their last five Segunda División matches, is closely counterbalanced by Sporting Gijón's resilient away form, including draws against top sides like Espanyol and Leganés. Recent developments show no major injuries reported on official team sheets, with both squads at near-full strength—Racing relying on forward Andrés Martín's scoring streak, while Gijón's Nikola Pavlovic anchors midfield effectively. Head-to-head history is tight, with three draws in the last five encounters, fueling trader consensus on a low-scoring stalemate possibility. Momentum from Racing's midweek rest versus Gijón's fatigue adds uncertainty, keeping implied probabilities bunched amid promotion stakes.

question: Counter-Strike: 3DMAX vs FOKUS REALITY (BO1) - Urban Riga Open #3 Group A
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike match between 3DMAX and FOKUS REALITY in the Urban Riga Open #3 Group A, initially scheduled for March 23 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "3DMAX" if 3DMAX win the match against FOKUS REALITY.

This market will resolve to "FOKUS REALITY" if FOKUS REALITY win the match against 3DMAX.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus in the 3DMAX vs FOKUS REALITY BO1 matchup at Urban Riga Open #3 Group A leans toward 3DMAX as slight favorites, reflecting their stronger recent form in Tier 2 CS2 circuits with a 60% win rate over the last 10 maps versus FOKUS REALITY's struggles, including three straight losses. No confirmed roster changes or stand-ins reported from official HLTV or team channels, keeping lineups stable—3DMAX's rifler Maka boasts a 1.15 rating, edge in clutches. BO1 format amplifies map veto volatility; watch Ancient or Mirage preferences from head-to-head (3DMAX 2-1). Group stage pressure adds upset potential, with both needing wins for playoffs.

question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

Esport BERG, languishing near #900, dropped a 0-2 to mid-tier foes recently, hampered by inconsistent AWPers and lineup flux after a key rifler departure. BO1 volatility favors upsets via pistol rounds, but AaB's home-region rest advantage and head-to-head edge from prior qualifiers tilt odds firmly their way amid group advancement stakes.

question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

question: Will the highest temperature in London be 9°C or below on March 23?
description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the London City Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 23 Mar '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the London City Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/gb/london/EGLC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus strongly favors a 15°C high in London on March 23 at 49% implied probability, propelled by the latest Met Office and ECMWF forecasts projecting maximums of 14-16°C under a persistent high-pressure ridge ushering mild southerly winds from the Atlantic. March climatology averages 11-12°C at Heathrow, but above-normal 500 hPa geopotential heights signal continued warmth, corroborated by recent observations of mid-teens highs earlier this week. GFS ensembles reinforce this clustering around 15°C, though low-level cloud could cap extremes; traders monitor 12Z model updates for any cold front risks nudging odds toward 14°C.

eventMetadata.context_description: The razor-thin margin between 28°C (34.5%) and 29°C (32.5%) in trader odds for São Paulo's March 23 peak temperature stems from converging short-range forecasts from INMET and global models like GFS and ECMWF, projecting highs of 28-29°C under a stable high-pressure ridge with light southerly winds moderating earlier warmth. Partial cloud cover and elevated humidity—evident in recent soundings—cap extremes, while urban heat island amplification in the metro area introduces a slim upside risk toward 29°C. Historical March averages hover at 27.5°C, with diurnal peaks typically between 3-5 PM; low probabilities for 30°C+ reflect ensemble consensus against prolonged insolation, though final NWP runs could sway sentiment.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment hinges on ensemble weather model forecasts from ECMWF and GFS, which converge on a Seoul high of 15-16°C on March 23, driven by a high-pressure ridge ushering mild southerly winds and ample solar insolation after recent cold snaps. The Korea Meteorological Administration's latest outlook aligns closely, projecting 14-16°C amid low cloud cover, boosting 16°C+ odds to 42.8% over 15°C at 35.5%. Differentiation stems from model spread in boundary layer mixing and afternoon convective heating—ECMWF leans warmer, while GFS shows slight cooling from upstream moisture. Historical March highs average 12°C but vary ±4°C with synoptic patterns; upcoming 12Z model runs could shift consensus.

This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Incheon Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 23 Mar '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Incheon Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/kr/incheon/RKSI.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment hinges on ensemble weather model forecasts from ECMWF and GFS, which converge on a Seoul high of 15-16°C on March 23, driven by a high-pressure ridge ushering mild southerly winds and ample solar insolation after recent cold snaps. The Korea Meteorological Administration's latest outlook aligns closely, projecting 14-16°C amid low cloud cover, boosting 16°C+ odds to 42.8% over 15°C at 35.5%. Differentiation stems from model spread in boundary layer mixing and afternoon convective heating—ECMWF leans warmer, while GFS shows slight cooling from upstream moisture. Historical March highs average 12°C but vary ±4°C with synoptic patterns; upcoming 12Z model runs could shift consensus.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent Environment Canada and ECMWF model ensembles point to a daytime high near 2°C in Toronto on March 23, driven by a deep upper trough funneling Arctic air southward via northerly winds, underpinning the 34% trader consensus for 2°C over 3°C at 26.5%. Differentiating factors include potential cloud cover modulating insolation—favoring 3-4°C if stratus thickens—or clearer skies reinforcing sub-2°C outcomes amid radiative cooling. Historical March 23 highs average 5°C, but this anomalously cool pattern, with 850mb temps -10°C, caps upside; 1°C and below hold slim odds barring surprise reinforcement. Traders eye 00z GFS updates for front timing shifts impacting final probabilities.
